other_observers: Andrew Spencer, Walter Szeliga, Roger Linfield; Larry Arnold found the bird. I know that Coen Dexter, Brenda Wright, Forrest Luke, Nic Korte, Rich Levad, Vic Zerbi, and others saw it on the 24th. Mark Peterson, Brandon Percival, Rachel Hopper and others saw it on the 25th.
duration: 30 seconds first visit and 2 minutes second visit
specific_location: Larry Arnold's house, 308 Country Club Park Rd, Grand Junction
description: Size and shape like Lesser Goldfinch. Significantly smaller than House Finch in direct comparison. Bill was pale. Clean glossy black cap and very small black chin, very sharp borders to both. Most of bird pale pearl gray. White undertail and whitish undertail coverts. Bright yellow patch on lower breast and upper belly, in kind of an oval shape. Wings and upperide of tail dark, nearly black. Strong yellow highlights in wing, including a large patch in the primaries and most of the coverts, connected by a thin strip of yellow. Tail was slightly forked.Behaviors: Bird was visiting a thistle seed feeder, occasionally hanging upside down from the perch in order to reach the seed.Call: Bird was silent.Plumage: adult
